title: zephyranthes minuta
intro: Zephyranthes minuta is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, commonly known as the small Zephyr lily or miniature rain lily. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the Amazonian and Mexican folk medicine, for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Zephyranthes minuta include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a treatment for wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or infusion, or it is extracted into a tincture or oil for topical application.
